title: Outage troubleshooting workflow
summary: Collect impact scope, incident timeline, status-page state, and recent deployment context before escalating.
body: For production availability incidents, verify affected region, error class, customer impact count, incident start time, and current workaround. Attach status-page evidence and route confirmed production outages to engineering incident response.

title: Refund and duplicate charge handling
summary: Billing disputes require invoice verification and billing approval before written refund confirmation.
body: Support may acknowledge a billing concern and gather invoice details. Support must not promise a refund, credit, or reversal before billing approves the transaction review.

title: Enterprise SLA escalation
summary: Enterprise critical incidents inside the SLA window require immediate owner assignment and engineering or success escalation.
body: Critical or high-priority enterprise tickets approaching an SLA deadline must receive an explicit owner, urgency classification, escalation route, and customer update cadence.

title: Security and data deletion workflow
summary: Security-sensitive requests require identity verification and security escalation before confirmation.
body: Agents must verify requestor identity and account authority before deletion, access revocation, or security confirmation. Security operations owns the final action and audit trail.

title: Integration bug triage
summary: Integration failures should capture connector, auth changes, error payload, timestamps, and business impact.
body: For integration failures, collect connector name, recent credential changes, error codes, retry state, and deadline impact. Premium and enterprise integration blockers should route to engineering support.

title: Tone and customer communication policy
summary: Customer messages should be calm, specific, grounded in evidence, and avoid commitments that require approval.
body: Use direct language, acknowledge urgency, state what is known, identify the next action, cite evidence internally, and avoid unsupported commitments or legal, security, refund, or compensation promises.
